title: Replace dynamic map access with head destructuring (OrderFormatter.summary/1)
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.OrderFormatter.summary/1` from test_fixtures/map_access_noise.ex. The function uses `order[:id]`, `order[:items]`, `order[:total]`, and then inner `item[:name]`, `item[:quantity]`. Replace with head destructuring: `def summary(%{id: id, items: items, total: total})` and inside `Enum.map/2` use `fn %{name: name, quantity: qty} -> ... end`. Also add a catch-all clause returning `"Invalid order"`. You may also refactor `plot_point/1` in the same module. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.OrderFormatter do ... end` containing summary + plot_point only.

must_contain: def summary(%{id: id, items: items, total: total})
def plot_point(%{x: x, y: y, z: z})

tier_rationale: Two-function refactor requiring inner-lambda destructuring inside Enum.map. Sonnet often destructures the outer but not the inner.
